_doc: Open Enzyme chokepoint targets to map fungal compounds against. Derived from wiki/modality-chokepoint-matrix.md (canonical chokepoint inventory) + wiki/nlrp3-exploit-map.md (NLRP3 sub-chokepoints CP0-CP6) + wiki/abcg2-modulators.md (transporter biology) + wiki/complement-c5a-gout.md (CP0 complement layer). UniProt accessions are the join key for cross-database target mapping.
_phase_1_status: canonical chokepoint set + ONE proposed addition (redox/disulfide chokepoint) flagged for Phase 5 confirmation
_proposed_new_chokepoint: Per Brian's framing 2026-05-06: fungi are biochemically rich in thiol/disulfide-modulating compounds (ergothioneine, gliotoxin-class ETPs, glutathione analogs). The wiki's chokepoint inventory does not currently include redox/disulfide chemistry as a named chokepoint. comp-014 may surface enough fungal-compound coverage of this axis to warrant adding it as a formal chokepoint with its own modality-row of fermentable interventions. The DAF SCR1-4 disulfide-count incident (CLAUDE.md §Pre-commit verification gate) shows disulfide chemistry is already load-bearing for the engineering side; the patient-side biology has been less developed.

uricase_substrate.name: Uric acid degradation (luminal sink)
uricase_substrate.rationale: Not a protein target — substrate degradation. Fungal compounds are not direct uricase competitors but may include other purine-degrading enzymes or substrate-modifying chemistries. LOW priority for breadth pass (modality is enzymatic, not small-molecule).

URAT1.name: Renal urate reabsorption transporter
URAT1.rationale: Validated drug target (probenecid, benzbromarone, lesinurad, dotinurad). comp-013 found astilbin, luteolin, berberine all have URAT1 animal-model evidence. Fungal hits here are HIGH priority.

GLUT9.name: Renal/intestinal urate transporter
GLUT9.rationale: Less drugged than URAT1 but mechanistically validated.

ABCG2.name: Gut + renal urate efflux transporter
ABCG2.rationale: comp-004 supplement screen validated this target. Q141K variant (Brian-relevant) needs both inducers (PPARγ agonists) AND folding rescuers. Fungal pharmacological-chaperone candidates are an open exploration vector.

Xanthine_Oxidase.name: Xanthine → uric acid producer
Xanthine_Oxidase.rationale: Allopurinol, febuxostat target. comp-013 confirmed luteolin XO 550 nM. Fungal XO inhibitors are extensively reported in CNKI/Asian literature with limited Western coverage.

NLRP3.name: NLRP3 inflammasome (assembly)
NLRP3.rationale: Central gout-flare driver. β-glucan modulation of NLRP3 priming is a well-studied fungal-compound axis (turkey tail PSK, maitake D-fraction, lentinan).

ASC.name: ASC (PYCARD) inflammasome adaptor
ASC.rationale: Oridonin and related natural products block ASC oligomerization. Some fungal triterpenes may bind the PYD domain.

Caspase_1.name: Inflammasome effector caspase
Caspase_1.rationale: Downstream of NLRP3+ASC; pharmacological inhibition is a separate intervention class.

IL_1B.name: Pro-inflammatory cytokine (flare effector)
IL_1B.rationale: Canakinumab/anakinra target. Fungal compounds may modulate at transcription, processing, or signaling layers.

TNFA.name: TNFα (gut barrier / vessel-wall inflammation)
TNFA.rationale: Gut-barrier and vessel-wall inflammation cycle. Fungal hits often have anti-TNF effects via NF-κB modulation.

DAF_CD55.name: Decay-Accelerating Factor (complement regulator)
DAF_CD55.rationale: Open Enzyme is engineering CD55 SCR1-4 itself (comp-006/comp-012). NOT a target for fungal-compound binding — included for completeness in the chokepoint inventory.

C5aR1.name: C5a receptor (CP0 priming)
C5aR1.rationale: validation-experiments.md §1.21 already concluded ZERO validated natural-product C5aR1 antagonists exist after ChEMBL/NPASS/LOTUS/Open Targets scan. Fungal-only re-scan via comp-014 may surface candidates Open Targets missed (especially Asian-literature compounds). HIGH priority for the breadth pass — surfacing even one validated fungal C5aR1 antagonist would partially close the avacopan-dependence gap.

Lp_PLA2.name: Lp-PLA2 (vessel-wall inflammation, Brian-relevant biomarker)
Lp_PLA2.rationale: Brian's persistent elevation across panels. Fungal-compound modulation is unmapped territory.

HDAC6.name: HDAC6 (Q141K-ABCG2 trafficking rescue)
HDAC6.rationale: comp-007 ran the supplement-grade HDACi screen. Fungal compounds with HDAC6 selectivity are an open exploration class.

PPARG.name: PPARγ (ABCG2 induction via butyrate axis)
PPARG.rationale: Fungal triterpenoids (esp. ganoderic acids, lanostanes) often have PPARγ agonist activity.

NRF2_KEAP1.name: Nrf2/KEAP1 (oxidative stress / barrier integrity)
NRF2_KEAP1.rationale: Sulforaphane axis; fungal compounds (esp. triterpenoids, melanins) often activate Nrf2.

OAT1_OAT3.name: Renal organic anion transporters
OAT1_OAT3.rationale: Off-target risk profile for any URAT1-targeted compound — needs counter-screen.

OAT4.name: OAT4 — apical urate exchanger (renal proximal tubule)
OAT4._canonical_wiki_gap: OAT4 is NOT in the canonical Open Enzyme wiki as of 2026-05-06 — Phase 1 peer review of comp-014 surfaced this as a wiki gap. OAT4 is a known benzbromarone target alongside URAT1 (apical urate exchanger in renal proximal tubule); it should be in abcg2-modulators.md or gout-pathophysiology.md but currently is not. Adding it here both for comp-014's intended scope and as a forcing function for the wiki gap to be closed in a separate sweep.
OAT4.rationale: Apical urate exchanger; benzbromarone hits this alongside URAT1. Off-target counter-screen relevance similar to OAT1/OAT3.

ADA.name: Adenosine Deaminase — purine catabolism upstream of XO
ADA._added_via_comp_014_phase_5: Promoted from implicit-downstream-of-XO to first-class chokepoint based on G. lucidum GLPP polysaccharide-peptide evidence (PMID 36385640): 40.6% UA reduction in HUA mice with ADA as load-bearing mechanism. ADA is biochemically canonical purine catabolism (adenosine → inosine → hypoxanthine → xanthine → UA); inhibiting it reduces flux into the XO substrate pool.
ADA.rationale: Druggable, biochemically distinct from XO (allopurinol/febuxostat) and uricase (rasburicase). Existing pentostatin-class inhibitors are chemotoxicity-grade — fungal natural-product ADA inhibitors (e.g., GLPP polysaccharide-peptide, structurally distinct from nucleoside analogs) are an open exploration vector with potentially cleaner toxicity profile.
ADA.phase_5b_action: Add to modality-chokepoint-matrix.md as new column under urate-axis grouping; add to gout-pathophysiology.md and abcg2-modulators.md.

_PRELIMINARY_PINK1_mitophagy.name: PINK1 / Mitophagy (PRELIMINARY — single paper, NLRP3-priming-adjacent)
_PRELIMINARY_PINK1_mitophagy.rationale: Single-paper PRELIMINARY-tier chokepoint candidate per Phase 1 peer-review-recommended three-tier rubric. PMID 40334761 (Cordyceps cicadae) shows PINK1-mediated mitophagy modulation in HUA nephropathy. Mechanism connects to NLRP3 priming via mitochondrial-dysfunction-induced ROS — folds naturally into the existing NLRP3 sub-chokepoint map.
_PRELIMINARY_PINK1_mitophagy.promotion_criteria: ≥3 additional papers from independent groups demonstrating PINK1/mitophagy modulation by fungal compounds with HUA / NLRP3 outcomes → promote to canonical chokepoint.
_PRELIMINARY_PINK1_mitophagy.current_action: Track in chokepoint-targets.json with _PRELIMINARY_ prefix; do NOT propagate to canonical wiki pages.

_REJECTED_redox_disulfide_modulators.name: Redox/disulfide chemistry — REJECTED 2026-05-06 (Phase 5 verdict)
_REJECTED_redox_disulfide_modulators._phase_5_verdict: REJECT per the three-tier rubric. Breadth pass surfaced essentially zero direct evidence base for a standalone redox/disulfide chokepoint. PubMed Phase 2c had one entry under ergothioneine_thioredoxin_TXNIP and that entry was empty. Phase 5 GLPP deep-read confirmed redox chemistry exists in fungal compounds (Keap1/Nrf2/HO-1 via GLPP — PMC11351902) but the redox axis is NOT the load-bearing claim for any HUA outcome; it's a sister-paper finding, decoupled from the urate-axis chokepoints comp-014 is mapping. Action: fold redox/disulfide-modulating fungal compounds into the existing NLRP3 sub-chokepoint map as a 'redox/Keap1-Nrf2 priming axis' rather than elevating to a standalone chokepoint. TXNIP target stays under NLRP3 axis, not standalone.
_REJECTED_redox_disulfide_modulators._re_evaluation_trigger: Future comp-NNN extending to marine fungi (per medicinal-mushroom-compound-mapping-computational.md §6.9 limitation) surfacing ETP-class compounds (gliotoxin, sirodesmin, chetomin family) with disulfide-bridge-mediated activity at ≥3 mechanistic classes → re-open. Terrestrial-fungi-only scope is the binding constraint.
_REJECTED_redox_disulfide_modulators._originally_proposed_status: PROPOSED 2026-05-06 by comp-014 scope. Not yet added to wiki/modality-chokepoint-matrix.md or wiki/nlrp3-exploit-map.md. Phase 5 deliverable: confirm or reject the chokepoint-warrant claim based on what fungal-compound coverage actually surfaces.

candidate_targets.name: Protein Disulfide Isomerase
candidate_targets.rationale: ER-resident; load-bearing for disulfide-rich therapeutic protein folding (DAF SCR1-4 engineering directly depends on this — see CLAUDE.md DAF disulfide incident).

candidate_targets.name: Thioredoxin
candidate_targets.rationale: Plasma + intracellular redox buffering; modulates NLRP3 priming via TXNIP axis.

candidate_targets.name: TXNIP (Thioredoxin-Interacting Protein)
candidate_targets.rationale: Direct NLRP3 activator under oxidative stress. Connects redox state → inflammasome priming. **Bidirectional relevance:** if fungal compounds suppress TXNIP, they suppress NLRP3 priming — connects redox chokepoint to NLRP3 chokepoint.

candidate_targets.name: ergothioneine biosynthesis (fungal-side)
candidate_targets.rationale: Fungal Egt1/Egt2 enzymes; not a human target. Listed for the producer-side: oyster mushroom and koji are ergothioneine-rich. If we want to elevate ergothioneine as a chokepoint-relevant compound, the producer organisms matter.

_REJECTED_redox_disulfide_modulators.rationale_for_inclusion: Fungi are biochemically distinctive in thiol/disulfide chemistry (ergothioneine biosynthesis is essentially fungal-and-mycobacterial; epipolythiodioxopiperazine ETPs like gliotoxin are fungal-specific). A breadth-first compound aggregation across fungi will surface this chemistry whether or not we ask for it. The choice is whether to map it onto a named chokepoint or let it appear as 'unmapped target space.' Adding it to the formal chokepoint inventory (Phase 5 decision) makes future breadth passes able to flag it directly.

phase_5_verdict_tiers.ADMIT: ≥20 fungal compounds with annotated activity across ≥3 mechanistic classes — formal chokepoint addition to modality-chokepoint-matrix.md as a new column.
phase_5_verdict_tiers.PRELIMINARY: 10-19 fungal compounds across ≥2 mechanistic classes — provisional column in the matrix with explicit 'needs Phase 5b expansion' tag. Phase 1 peer review (2026-05-06) recommended this intermediate tier to avoid binary admit/reject when the evidence is on the boundary.
phase_5_verdict_tiers.REJECT: Fewer than 10 fungal compounds OR dominated by one mechanistic class — fold the surfaced compounds into the existing NLRP3 sub-chokepoint map as an additional priming axis (TXNIP/redox), without elevating to a standalone chokepoint.
